question.text: Which of the following is a Category I AIF under SEBI (AIF) Regulations 2012?

answer.text: Debt Fund investing in listed NCDs

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Category I AIFs invest in sectors considered socially or economically desirable by the government/regulators. They include venture capital funds, SME funds, social impact funds, special situation funds, infrastructure funds, and CDMDF. Social Impact Funds promote social welfare and are explicitly under Category I. **The Trap:** Hedge Funds are Category III (complex trading strategies, leverage). Private Equity Funds and Debt Funds fall under Category II (no special incentives, no leverage at fund level). **Cross-Exam Context:** AIF categorization is a core concept tested across all XIX-series exams. The distinction is also relevant for taxation (Chapter 12) as categories have different tax treatments. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Category I AIFs may enjoy government incentives and concessions — use this as a differentiator when presenting VCF and infrastructure fund opportunities to tax-sensitive investors.
